title: Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Edge Handling

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable merge sort algorithm. Handle edge cases where the array has less than 2 elements or is already sorted.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a list of integers
statement.outputFormat: Output is a sorted list of integers
statement.notes: The implementation should be stable, meaning equal elements maintain their original order.

examples.explanation: Sort the array in ascending order
Sort the array in ascending order and maintain equal elements' original order

hints.text: Check the base cases and edge cases handling in the merge sort algorithm

editorial.title: Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Edge Handling
editorial.intuition: Implement a stable merge sort algorithm
editorial.bruteForce: Use a naive implementation of merge sort
editorial.betterSolution: Use a divide-and-conquer approach with recursion
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a stable merge sort algorithm with edge cases handling
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The algorithm is stable and correct
commonMistakes: Stable merge sort algorithm not implemented

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]:
        if len(nums) < 2: return nums
        mid = len(nums) // 2
        left = nums[:mid]
        right = nums[mid:]
        left = self.mergeSort(left)
        right = self.mergeSort(right)
        i = 0; j = 0
        for k in range(len(nums)):
            if i < len(left) and (j == len(right) or left[i] <= right[j]):
                nums[k] = left[i]
                i += 1
            else:
                nums[k] = right[j]
                j += 1
        return nums

    def mergeSort(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]:
        if len(nums) < 2: return nums
        mid = len(nums) // 2
        left = nums[:mid]
        right = nums[mid:]
        left = self.mergeSort(left)
        right = self.mergeSort(right)
        i = 0; j = 0
        while i < len(left) and j < len(right):
            if left[i] <= right[j]:
                nums[i+j] = left[i]
                i += 1
            else:
                nums[i+j] = right[j]
                j += 1
        while i < len(left):
            nums[i+j] = left[i]
            i += 1
        while j < len(right):
            nums[i+j] = right[j]
            j += 1
        return nums
